title: Compiler Overview
bodyMarkdown:
Short overview of the compiler that produces the kernels Helios serves, for engineers whose work touches serving or restore but not the compiler itself.

## What it does
Takes a model graph and lowers it to fused kernels tuned for our hardware. Fusion decisions affect both throughput and memory footprint, which is why serving capacity planning sometimes needs compiler input.

## Relationship to serving
The compiler is a separate build step, not something serving invokes at request time. A model is compiled once, the output artifact is what src/serving actually loads and runs. If a kernel is misbehaving in production, the fix almost always lands in the compiler repo, not in src/serving.

## Common failure modes
Op ordering bugs in fused kernels are the most frequent compiler issue that surfaces downstream, usually as a subtle correctness problem rather than a crash. Out-of-memory during compilation of very large batch graphs is the second most common, distinct from any runtime OOM.

## Where to ask questions
Jonah Kim owns the compiler. For anything that looks compiler-shaped, tag him early rather than debugging deep into serving first.
